title: Doctor skill description lint treats CRLF SKILL frontmatter as missing
description: fabric-doctor reports fabric-archive/fabric-review/fabric-import as missing YAML frontmatter even though the installed and packaged SKILL.md files start with frontmatter bytes `---\r\nname:`. The lint uses an LF-only regex, unlike neighboring doctor frontmatter parsers that accept `\r?\n`, so Windows-normalized CRLF skill files become deterministic false positives for skill_description_quality.
fix_direction: Change inspectSkillDescription to use the shared CRLF/BOM-tolerant frontmatter regex (`/^(?:\uFEFF)?---\r?\n([\s\S]*?)\r?\n---/u`) and add CRLF SKILL.md regression coverage for description lint.

context.notes: Discovered by Round 5 dedup + issue creation worker DBP-20260609-033400

issue_history.note: Registered by Round 5 dedup + issue creation worker

title: Legacy archive-hint.cjs files remain under installed hook trees after the fabric-hint rename
description: The current installed trees still contain .claude/hooks/archive-hint.cjs and .codex/hooks/archive-hint.cjs even though packages/cli/templates/hooks has no archive-hint.cjs source and install now copies fabric-hint.cjs. The install sweep only strips config entries by basename before merge; it does not delete the stale script files, and fabric-doctor.txt reports cross-client content drift for archive-hint.cjs. These orphan scripts preserve retired local knowledge/pending logic and create noisy deterministic drift in installed hook audits.
fix_direction: Extend the rc27 cleanup to remove legacy archive-hint.cjs files from .claude/.codex/.cursor hook directories during install, or explicitly exclude retired basenames from hook-content drift checks after config sweep. Add an install regression that seeds archive-hint.cjs files and asserts they are gone after reinstall.

context.notes: Discovered by Round 5 dedup + issue creation worker DBP-20260609-033400

issue_history.note: Registered by Round 5 dedup + issue creation worker

title: Canonical fabric-archive SKILL exceeds the doctor hot-path token budget
description: fabric-doctor reports fabric-archive at 5721 estimated tokens, and the packaged template plus installed copies are 17162 chars / 171 lines. This is not just local state: packages/cli/templates/skills/fabric-archive/SKILL.md ships the over-budget hot-path body, so fresh installs inherit the warning and extra model-load cost.
fix_direction: Move detailed phase mechanics, examples, and long MUST/NEVER catalogues into ref/ files and leave SKILL.md with the trigger gate plus concise phase index. Keep token-budget CI/doctor coverage on the canonical template, not only installed copies.

context.notes: Discovered by Round 5 dedup + issue creation worker DBP-20260609-033400

issue_history.note: Registered by Round 5 dedup + issue creation worker

title: Store scope lint never verifies visibility_store matches the physical holding store
description: entryScopeMetadataSchema defines visibility_store as the alias/UUID of the store where an entry physically lives, and write-side code stamps target.alias. lintStoreScopes reads visibility_store but only checks whether it is missing; the subsequent checks cover personal leaks and dangling project refs, not whether visibility_store equals the actual read-set store alias/UUID. A canonical team entry physically in store A with visibility_store: "B" can pass doctor scope lint, leaving stale provenance invisible even though recall/citation surfaces depend on store provenance.
fix_direction: Add a visibility_store_mismatch violation comparing the parsed visibility_store against the current StoreCtx alias and/or uuid, include it in createScopeLintCheck breakdown/remediation, and add a regression where an entry in the team store declares another store alias.

context.notes: Discovered by Round 5 dedup + issue creation worker DBP-20260609-033400

issue_history.note: Registered by Round 5 dedup + issue creation worker

title: by-alias best-effort symlink failures turn into permanent doctor drift warnings
description: syncStoreAliasLinks creates ~/.fabric/stores/by-alias before attempting symlinkSync and swallows symlink failures as best-effort errors. detectAliasLinkDrift suppresses drift only when the by-alias directory does not exist; once doctor --fix or store mutation has created the directory on a platform/account that cannot create symlinks, missing links are reported forever with a remediation that cannot succeed. The test suite also returns early when sync.errors includes the alias, so this failure mode is not asserted.
fix_direction: Persist or infer a symlink-unsupported state and make detectAliasLinkDrift return [] when sync cannot create links, or avoid creating by-alias until at least one link succeeds. Add a test that forces syncStoreAliasLinks to error and then asserts doctor/detectAliasLinkDrift stays quiet or reports an actionable unsupported-state message instead of repairable drift.

context.notes: Discovered by Round 5 dedup + issue creation worker DBP-20260609-033400

issue_history.note: Registered by Round 5 dedup + issue creation worker

title: Discovery catalog index omits every completed DBP discovery run
description: .workflow/issues/discoveries/index.json still contains only the old DSC-20260421-184152 entry, while the discoveries directory contains 15 completed DBP-* runs with discovery-state.json and discovery-issues.jsonl up through ISS-20260609-059. Consumers that rely on the catalog index cannot discover or validate the generated multi-round artifacts, so registry artifact integrity checks silently miss the live DBP history.
fix_direction: Update the discovery completion/write path to append or upsert DBP runs into index.json, or replace the static index with a derived directory scan. Add an integrity check that every completed discovery-state.json has a corresponding index entry and that current in-progress runs are explicitly excluded.

context.notes: Discovered by Round 5 dedup + issue creation worker DBP-20260609-033400
